channel.title: 2026-05-30 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-30 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Physics-Informed Descriptors Base Machine Learning Model for High-Precision (Molecular Property Dipole Moment(μ)) Prediction on QM9
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Mirza Mudassar Hussain, Uzma Ahmad, Saira Hameed, Fikadu Tasgera Tolesa
Dipole Moment(µ) is important property which helps to understand how molecules behave and support to design the new material. Therefore, predicting a molecule’s dipole moment property has high importance. In this paper, we introduce a MLP Model based on physics-informed descriptors which help to predict dipole moment µ on QM9 dataset with a test MAE = 0.0231Debye. Our selection of descriptor (atom counts, bond lengths, bond angles, and approximate partial charge) helps this model to learn more and produces highly precise results without using 3D methods. Above mentioned results show this model is fast, precise and accurate alternative to existing 3D − GNN models which are complex and required complete 3D geometry. This model performs better then GNN models such as ALIGNN(MAE = 0.0248Debye…

item.title: Smart Electric Vehicle Energy Consumption Prediction Using Hybrid Physics–Machine Learning Approach with Route and Weather Integration
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Nirmitha S, Kavita V. Horadi
Electric vehicles (EVs) are becoming a key solution for sustainable transportation; however, accurately predicting their energy consumption under diverse real-world conditions remains a complex challenge. The majority of current methods either only use physics-based models, which are not flexible, or machine learning models, which need large amounts of real-world data. In order to get around these restrictions, this study suggests a hybrid EV energy prediction framework that integrates physics-based modeling with machine learning, while also incorporating real-world environmental and road conditions. The proposed system considers critical factors such as vehicle mass, speed, auxiliary load, and aerodynamic forces, along with road characteristics including slope (uphill and downhill), surf…

item.title: Earthquake Magnitude Estimation Using Machine Learning from Single Station Seismic Data in Rwanda
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 UWAMUNGU Dany, NIZEYIMANA Eric, NIZEYIMANA Pacifique, HAGUMUWUMVA David
Earthquake magnitude estimation is critical for seismic monitoring, particularly in data-constrained regions with sparse station coverage. This study investigates the use of machine learning techniques for rapid and reliable magnitude estimation from single-station seismic waveform data recorded at the HUYE station in Rwanda, located within the western branch of the East African Rift System. A machine learning framework was developed using three-second waveform windows centered on P-wave arrivals. Both raw waveform inputs and physics-informed engineered features were utilized, including logarithmic amplitude, cumulative displacement, signal energy, root-mean-square (RMS) amplitude, signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), dominant frequency, spectral centroid, and band-limited spectral energy (1–10 H…

item.title: Phase Accumulation for Machine Learning Readoutin Graphene Terahertz Resonators
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Álvaro Garay
This work presents a phase-accumulation-based machinelearning readout for dynamically perturbed grapheneterahertz resonators. Instead of relying on steady-state or peakonlyobservables, the proposed approach integrates the transientphase response to construct physically meaningful features thatcapture both perturbation magnitude and temporal behavior. Aphysics-driven synthetic dataset is generated using a reducedorderdynamic model, incorporating parameter variability andnoise. Multiple feature configurations are evaluated for classificationand regression tasks. The results show that accumulatedphasedescriptors alone are insufficient, but significantly improveperformance when combined with instantaneous features, leadingto enhanced robustness and predictive accuracy, particularlyunder low s…

item.title: 通过反铁磁绝缘体实现的磁振子介导轨道力矩翻转
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Hui Yang, Zehan Chen, Weikai Luo, Xinxing Xu, Qianwen Wang, Peigang Han, Ji Shi, Hongyu An, and Kazuya Ando
该工作研究反铁磁绝缘体中由磁振子介导的轨道力矩传输及其对磁化翻转的作用。题名表明作者可能展示了轨道角动量、磁振子激发与自旋轨道器件开关行为之间的耦合关系；具体材料体系、实验构型和翻转效率需查阅原文。

item.title: 用于范德华自旋电子学的近室温或高于室温二维铁磁 Fe-M-Te（M = Ge, Ga）化合物
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Gaojie Zhang, Wen Jin, Hao Wu, Li Yang, Wenfeng Zhang, Kostya S. Novoselov, and Haixin Chang
该研究围绕二维 Fe-M-Te（M = Ge, Ga）范德华化合物的铁磁性展开，重点关注其在近室温或高于室温条件下的磁有序特征。此类材料若具备稳定二维铁磁性，将有望用于范德华自旋电子器件；具体合成方法、磁性表征和居里温度等信息需参阅原文。

item.title: Extrinsic anomalous Hall effect in altermagnets
item.description:
📚 npj Quantum Materials
👤 Alexander Osin, Alex Levchenko, Maxim Khodas
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 30 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00895-3 Extrinsic anomalous Hall effect in altermagnets

item.title: Linear magneto-birefringence as a probe of altermagnetism
item.description:
📚 npj Quantum Materials
👤 V. Sunko, J. Orenstein
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 30 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00901-8 Linear magneto-birefringence as a probe of altermagnetism

item.title: Diamond quantum sensor could reveal elusive altermagnets
item.description:
📚 Physics News
For nearly a century, there were two known kinds of magnets. Ferromagnets are the classic magnets that attract metal and keep pictures stuck to the refrigerator. Antiferromagnets hide their magnetism at the atomic scale but are increasingly prized for their technological potential. A third category discovered within the last decade may combine the best qualities of both. Dubbed altermagnets, they could someday help create faster, more energy-efficient electronics.

item.title: Supercurrent effect in a charge density wave intertwined superconductor
item.description:
📚 Nature Communications
👤 Zhen Zhu, Wei Cheng, Dang Liu, Pengyu Hu, Yi Yang, Qiaoyan Yu 等18位作者
Nature Communications, Published online: 30 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73778-7 Scanning tunneling spectroscopic imaging reveals a field-driven symmetry breaking of CDW modulations in superconducting NbSe₂. Model calculations suggest that the observed anisotropy originates from a Doppler-shift-induced reconstruction of the dispersion of Bogoliubov quasiparticle excitations.

item.title: 基于图注意力的多智能体深度确定性策略梯度用于多无人机协同包围与捕获
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Qiang Ge, Yimin Xu, Rui Xiao, Junjie Wei, Mengzhen Gan, Yangzhao Lan
该研究面向多无人机协同包围与捕获任务，提出基于图注意力的多智能体深度确定性策略梯度算法 GA-MADDPG。针对传统方法难以表达不同无人机时变重要性、动态交互复杂性以及奖励稀疏和训练非平稳的问题，作者将任务形式化为离散时间部分可观测马尔可夫博弈，并设计融合距离推进、角度覆盖和安全约束的分层奖励函数。方法还利用通信约束构建动态图结构，以刻画无人机之间随时间变化的拓扑关系。

item.title: Evidence for Spin Polarization and Lattice Oxygen Migration During Cyclohexane Oxidation Over CoOx/Fe2O3
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Xixi Zhang, Shichao Zhao, Shuangfeng Xing, Xingchen Liu, Chengyuan Liu, Bin Zhang, Zhuo Li, Wentao Hao, Panzhe Qiao, Conghui Wang, Yong Qin
Angewandte Chemie International Edition, EarlyView.

item.title: Development of Sub‐50 nm Core–Shell Silica Nanoparticles With Controlled In Vivo Behavior for 19F Magnetic Resonance Imaging
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Yue Wu, Masafumi Minoshima, Kazuya Kikuchi
Angewandte Chemie International Edition, EarlyView.

item.title: Selective Phonon-Excitation Controls Transient Magnetization in Monolayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Jinhong Xu, Youyou Tu, Zhenfa Zheng, Qijing Zheng, Jin Zhao
Ultrafast control of transient magnetization in non-magnetic materials is a central challenge for next-generation spintronics. In monolayer transition-metal dichalcogenides, optically induced magnetization is typically short-lived due to rapid carrier relaxation, where phonons are conventionally regarded as a dissipative background. Here, we show that phonons can instead act as active control knobs by selectively reshaping non-equilibrium carrier dynamics. Using first-principles momentum-resolved quantum simulations, we demonstrate that excitation of long-wavelength longitudinal acoustic phonons in monolayer MoS2 induces a pronounced asymmetry between electron and hole relaxation. This selectively accelerates electron spin equilibration via Elliott–Yafet-type scattering while leaving hole…

item.title: Characterization of a Heaterless 60 A Hollow Cathode on Krypton and Argon
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Naia Butler-Craig, Dan Lev, Mitchell L.R. Walker
We present a steady-state current-voltage (IV) characterization of a heaterless version of the 9 kW-class H9 Hall thruster cathode. This work was conducted at Georgia Tech’s High-Power Electric Propulsion Laboratory (HPEPL) in Vacuum Test Facility 2 at operational background pressures of 0.22–0.70 µTorr on krypton and 0.11–0.24 µTorr on argon, with a base pressure of 9.1 × 10− 8 Torr, and mapped steady-state voltage behavior from 5 to 35 A of discharge current on krypton and 5 to 20 A on argon, in both magnetized and unmagnetized configurations. The magnetized configuration produced a higher discharge voltage than the unmagnetized configuration at every paired (current, flow) operating point on both propellants. On krypton, the magnetized-unmagnetized voltage gap ranged from near zero at…

item.title: Shape Adaptive Radar Cross Section Reduction (RCSR) metastructure for Ku-Band
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Debasish Das, Nidhi Bhattacharyya
This work realizes an engineered metastructure in Ku-band for radar cross-section (RCS) reduction under different curvature states. The metastructure is designed by periodically embedding octagonal shaped subwavelength EG-PUR resonators within the dielectric matrix to create electromagnetic traps, enabling more than 90% reduction. An angular stability up to 70° for both TE and TM polarizations is achieved. A peak RCS reduction (RCSR) exceeding 20 dB is observed for both co- and cross-polarizations in reference to a completely reflecting surface. Monostatic RCS measurements reaffirm the performance of the metastructure in effectively reducing scattering in 12–18 GHz range. The RCSR remains unaffected under mechanical deformation, confirming its suitability for conformal applications. The p…

item.title: Theoretical Regime Map of Magnetic MultipoleModes in Coupled Split-Ring ResonatorMetamolecules
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Álvaro Garay
This work presents a compact theoretical framework for classifying magnetic multipole regimes in coupled split-ring resonator (SRR) metamolecules. Each SRR is modeled as an effective resonant magnetic element, while the collective response is described through the coupling ratio ḩi=|M|/L, the normalized modal splitting Søₘₑgₐ, the dipolar cancellation factor Γ_D, the reduced quadrupolar indicator Γ_Q, and the toroidal-like circulation factor Γ_T. A reference four-SRR metamolecule is used to distinguish independent SRRs, bright collective dipoles, dark dipole-suppressed states, quadrupolar-like configurations, and toroidal-like magnetic arrangements. The results show that modal splitting is controlled by ḩi, while multipolar behavior appears when the net magnetic dipole is suppressed. The…

item.title: Tough and Healable Shape‐Memory Elastomers Enabled by Dynamic Hyperbranched Topological Networks
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Zhao Xu, Juan Chen, Haili Qin, Jiang‐Qi Feng, Pan Guo, Hong Chen, Huai‐Ping Cong
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Calcium Salts of Biobased Anionic Surfactant Result in Hydrophilic, Air-Stable Bilayers by Distorted Hydrogen-Bond Networks
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Taichi Habe, Tadashi Sugahara, Hiroko Endo, Kana Tsumura, Atsushi Miyazaki, Kenta Motobayashi 等10位作者
Biobased hydroxy alkane sulfonate (HAS) caters to the growing demand for anionic surfactants that are eco-friendly, exert mild effects on humans, and remain effective in hard-water containing divalent cations. In contrast to surfaces treated with other surfactants, which become hydrophobic, this study shows that surfaces treated by HAS with C18 hydrocarbon chains (C18HAS) remain hydrophilic after drying owing to bilayer formation. Quartz crystal microbalance with dissipation measurements reveals that the formation of the C18HAS membrane follows the Langmuir adsorption kinetics. The adsorption of water molecules to the C18HAS layer was monitored under various humidity conditions using polarization-modulation infrared reflection absorption spectroscopy, suggesting that the adsorbed water mo…

item.title: 利用机器学习原子间势计算固体中的非谐自由能
item.description:
📚 ACS
👤 Jing Ma, Liying An, Huan Ma, Jinjia Liu, Wenping Guo, and Xiaodong Wen
该工作聚焦固体非谐自由能的计算问题，探讨机器学习原子间势在高精度、高效率热力学模拟中的应用。题名表明研究可能涉及以机器学习势替代昂贵的第一性原理采样，从而描述晶格非谐效应及其对相稳定性的影响；具体方法流程、验证体系和精度评估需查阅原文。

item.title: Emulsion-forcespun hollow polymer fibers as functional sorbents for uranium removal from water
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Mahmoud Firouzzare, Roshanak Rostamian
This study developed hollow amidoximated styrene–acrylonitrile (SAN) fibers using a novel emulsion-assisted solution forcespinning strategy for enhanced uranium adsorption. The fabricated hollow SAN fibers, with their unique core architecture, provided superior surface accessibility and faster mass transfer compared to solid fibers. This resulted in excellent uranium adsorption performance, achieving a maximum capacity of 190 mg g-1, approximately 1.4 times higher than that of solid fibers. This work presents an innovative approach to fabricate high-performance adsorbents for efficient recovery of uranium from aqueous solutions.

item.title: Bridging Synthesis and Device Performance in Perovskite Quantum Dot Light‐Emitting Diodes
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Zi‐Hao Zhang, Wan‐Shan Shen, Ya‐Kun Wang, Liang‐Sheng Liao
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.
